rules: RULE-01: At the start of every tick, any unit with poison>0 takes 5 poison damage and its poison duration decreases by 1.
RULE-02: A unit that is not stunned gains gauge equal to its speed every tick.
RULE-03: A unit acts when its gauge >= 100. Priority order is Player first, then Enemy.
RULE-04: ATTACK: if |player.pos - enemy.pos| <= 1 and energy >= 20, the target's hp -= 20 and the actor's energy -= 20 (hit); otherwise it fails (no effect).
RULE-05: MOVE: step one cell toward the opponent; if the new pos is within 0..10 and energy >= 10, pos moves by the direction and energy -= 10; otherwise it fails.
RULE-07: Every action (hit or fail) reduces the actor's gauge by 100.
RULE-08: A stunned unit gains no gauge and cannot act; its stun duration decreases by 1 at tick end.
RULE-09: The game ends immediately when any unit's hp <= 0. winner is the surviving side ('player' or 'enemy'); if both <= 0, winner is 'draw'.
RULE-10: The game runs at most 50 ticks. If no unit has died by the end of tick 50, the game ends in a draw.

RULE-11 (THIS CARD): FATIGUE. At the very end of each tick, AFTER the stun-duration decrement, any unit whose energy is exactly 0 takes 3 fatigue damage; then apply the end check (RULE-09) again. Everything else stays byte-for-byte identical to before — a tick in which neither unit sits at energy 0 prints exactly as the previous card did.

files.description: CLI entry: parse --scenario N, run engine, print flattened state via format.render.

Tick loop: poison -> gauge gain -> actions (player then enemy) -> stun decrement. THIS card adds ONLY RULE-11 FATIGUE at tick end (energy==0 -> -3 hp, then end check). Keep all existing coupled mechanics intact. This is the ONLY module this card edits.
